title: ceropegia lucida subsp. dryophila
intro: Ceropegia lucida subsp. dryophila, also known as the 'Glistening Milk-pair' or 'Dryophila Milk-pair', is a rare and exotic species of flowering plant in the Apocynaceae family, native to tropical Africa. The plant has been reported to possess medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where it is used to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Ceropegia lucida subsp. dryophila include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help to soothe skin irritations and infections. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a herbal tea or infusion, as well as being used in topical applications such as creams and ointments.
